title: Upcoming behavior change: Choose entitlements when adding principals to workspaces
body:
Databricks is changing how principals get workspace entitlements. After this change, you grant entitlements explicitly when adding a principal to a workspace instead of relying on inheritance from the `users` system group. Workspace administrators can opt in starting **June 15, 2026**, and the new behavior is enforced for all workspaces on **September 14, 2026**.

This change enables you to add principals at any access level, including consumer-only users, without them automatically inheriting authoring privileges.

### What's changing

Every workspace has two system groups: `users`, which includes all principals granted access to the workspace, and `admins`, which includes the workspace administrators. Today, every principal added to a workspace inherits the entitlements granted to `users`. By default, these are:

* **Workspace access** — create and use notebooks, jobs, pipelines, apps, and more.
* **Databricks SQL access** — create and use dashboards, Genie Agents, alerts, and more.

After the change:

* The `users` group will have no entitlements. The `admins` group will have all workspace entitlements. Both groups' entitlements are locked.
* New principals must be granted entitlements explicitly when added to a workspace.
* `users` and `admins` cannot be nested as members of other groups.

Existing principals retain their current level of access. Databricks automatically migrates the entitlements previously granted to `users` to a new, workspace-local clone group named `users-clone-<TIMESTAMP>` (where `<TIMESTAMP>` is the time of migration). You manage the clone group like any other workspace-local group, and you can customize its name when you opt in early. The `admins` group doesn't require migration.

### Action required

* **If you manage system group entitlements through automation** (Terraform, Workspace SCIM APIs, or custom scripts), update your workflows to target standard account groups, not system groups. After the new behavior is enabled, attempts to modify system group entitlements will fail.
* **If `users` or `admins` is nested as a member of another group**, remove the nesting. Nesting is not permitted under the new behavior.
* **If your SCIM sync deletes workspace groups it doesn't recognize**, update its configuration to preserve the migration clone group (`users-clone-<TIMESTAMP>`). If the sync removes the clone group, principals migrated to it lose their entitlements.

### Timeline

* **June 15, 2026** – Opt-in available in workspace settings under **Advanced > Access control**.
* **July 27, 2026** – Auto-enabled for workspaces that haven't opted in or out. Opt-out remains available.
* **September 14, 2026** – New behavior enforced for all workspaces. Opt-out removed.

You manage the new behavior from your workspace settings under **Advanced > Access control**:

*Before opt-in: legacy behavior is active.*

*After opt-in or auto-enable: new behavior is active.*

For more information, see [Migrate workspace entitlement control](/aws/en/security/auth/system-group-entitlements-migration).
